expectations.text: Pushes back on the assumption respectfully
expectations.evidence: "I hear you, and I get the instinct — you know the investors are technical, so you want to speak their language. But this is actually one of the most common traps in pitch prep"

expectations.text: References the guide on plain English
expectations.evidence: "the YC guide calls it out directly" and "The guide puts it well: your two-sentence description should create a picture in the listener's mind."

expectations.text: Explains why clarity wins over technical depth at demo day
expectations.evidence: "The pitches that land are the ones that are instantly clear, not the ones that flex architecture knowledge." Also: "Demo Day is about getting that meeting" — frames the real goal clearly.

expectations.text: Does NOT just agree with the founder's premise
expectations.evidence: Clear, respectful pushback throughout. "So instead of making the pitch more technical, let's make it more concrete and specific. That's what actually impresses technical people — specificity, not jargon."

eval_feedback.overall: Excellent coaching. Uses the Stripe example effectively to show how a technical audience still benefits from clarity. Respectful pushback that reframes rather than dismisses. Ends with a concrete next step.
